project.name: Quill
project.description: Post-quantum encrypted P2P messenger. Classical asymmetric algorithms (RSA, ECDH) are vulnerable to Shor's algorithm on quantum computers. This project implements a fully quantum-safe communication protocol based on NIST PQC standards (FIPS 203, 204, 205) published in 2024.
project.future_use: Base for engineering thesis on intelligent PQC algorithm selection

priorities.primary: SECURITY — every decision must prioritize cryptographic correctness over convenience
priorities.secondary: CORRECTNESS — working crypto before any UI or optimization
priorities.tertiary: CLARITY — code must be readable and defensible in academic review
anti_goals: Do NOT reimplement crypto primitives — use liboqs and OpenSSL
Do NOT reuse nonces under any circumstance
Do NOT skip signature verification steps
Do NOT use classical asymmetric crypto (RSA, ECDH, DH) anywhere

done: liboqs built and integrated
Kyber-512/768/1024 KEM (encaps, decaps)
Dilithium/Falcon digital signatures
AES-256-GCM encrypt/decrypt with random nonce + optional AAD (context binding)
Replay protection for CHAT: per-direction monotonic sequence numbers bound via GCM AAD='CHAT|<seq>'; receiver rejects seq<=last (fail-closed); counters reset on every handshake/PFS rotation
TCP network layer (Socket, NetworkServer, NetworkClient)
Full PQC Handshake with Server/Client verification
ImGui Frontend (ChatApp, Theme) with Handshake visualization
Multi-client support via std::thread
Forward Secrecy (PFS) - key rotation
Security levels and benchmarking tools
HKDF-SHA256 session key derivation (salt = kyber_pub||ct, domain-separated info, RFC 5869 verified)
Identity management: persistent ML-DSA/Falcon keys, 0600/0700 perms, atomic write, SHA-3 fingerprint
Profiles + login: ProfileManager, QID2 (Argon2id + AES-GCM), QID1 migration, passphrase min 8 chars
Argon2 dual backend: OpenSSL >= 3.2 EVP_KDF or libargon2 on older distros (Ubuntu CI)
TOFU (TrustStore): known_hosts, UNVERIFIED/KNOWN/VERIFIED/MISMATCH, fail-closed on key change
CryptoManager: UI-free handshake + TOFU + HKDF + identity (quill_core)
File transfer: 64KB chunks, AES-GCM per chunk, AAD FILE|tid|index, chunk_hash SHA-3 per chunk, SHA-3 whole file in FILE_END
File transfer: selective repeat (FILE_NACK, max 5 rounds, FileSenderSession retransmit with new nonce)
File transfer UI: file picker, progress bars, download directory
ImGui git submodule (third_party/imgui v1.92.8)
GitHub Actions CI: build quill_core + ctest (QUILL_BUILD_GUI=OFF)
Google Test suite (tests/, 97 tests)
not_done: NAT Traversal: UDP hole punching + rendezvous server
Engineering thesis: intelligent PQC algorithm selection
Optional: Docker (build+test only), ASan/UBSan in CI, true E2E (hub redesign)
current_state.note: Feature-complete for semester project (v1.0.0). Everything from Quill_Prezentacja.pptx is implemented.

stack.crypto_symmetric: OpenSSL (Apache 2.0) — AES-256-GCM, SHA-3, HKDF, RAND_bytes; Argon2id via EVP_KDF (>=3.2) or libargon2
stack.ci: GitHub Actions — ubuntu-latest, liboqs built in job
stack.os: Arch Linux (primary dev), Ubuntu CI, target cross-platform

session_encryption.key_derivation: HKDF-SHA256(ikm=Kyber shared_secret, salt=kyber_pub||kem_ciphertext, info='quill-aes256gcm-session-v1|LEVEL') -> 32B AES key
session_encryption.nonce: 12 bytes, random per message via RAND_bytes — NEVER reuse
session_encryption.tag: 16 bytes GCM auth tag appended to ciphertext

critical_rules: Never reuse a nonce with the same key
Always verify PQC signatures before trusting received KEM material
Never fall back to classical crypto

protocol.transport: TCP with length-prefixed framing (uint32_t network byte order)

message_format.encoding: JSON via nlohmann/json (binary arrays for raw bytes)

protocol.file_transfer: FILE_START -> FILE_CHUNK (chunk_hash=SHA3 plaintext, AAD, AES-GCM) -> FILE_END (whole-file SHA3). Missing: FILE_NACK -> selective repeat.

architecture.concurrency: std::thread per client, std::mutex for shared state; m_security_level under mutex, m_msg_count atomic

directory_tree.src/crypto/: PQC, KDF, identity, profiles, TOFU, CryptoManager

ai_assistant_instructions.language: Respond in Polish unless asked otherwise
ai_assistant_instructions.focus: Security correctness above all else — always flag any crypto misuse
ai_assistant_instructions.style: Prefer clear, readable code over clever one-liners — this is academic code
ai_assistant_instructions.libs: Use liboqs for all PQC, OpenSSL for AES/SHA/RAND/HKDF — Argon2 via OpenSSL or libargon2
ai_assistant_instructions.context_awareness: Always refer to architecture.directory_tree when suggesting where to place new code.
